title: Cursor versus offset pagination
description: Offset paging is simple but skips or repeats items when the underlying data changes, while cursor paging stays stable across mutations.
body: Use when choosing a paging mechanism. Do not default to offset for large or frequently changing collections. Offset paging uses a numeric skip that shifts when rows are inserted or deleted between pages, causing gaps or duplicates; cursor paging encodes a position so iteration stays consistent. Inspect whether the data mutates during iteration. Validation signals include a client paging a changing collection without missing or repeating items. Risk boundary: offset paging on volatile data silently loses records, so prefer cursors when correctness during change matters.
